subject: Science
chapter: Exploring Substances: Acidic, Basic and Neutral

activities_projects.title: DIY pH Scale with Red Cabbage
activities_projects.objective: Create a wide-range natural indicator to identify pH levels
materials: Lemon, Vinegar, Baking soda, Soap, Tap water
steps: Boil shredded red cabbage to extract the purple pigment
Strain the liquid and let it cool
Add 20ml of indicator to each test substance
Arrange the resulting colors in a spectrum from red (acid) to green/yellow (base)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that indicators can show varying degrees of acidity and alkalinity
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate color-coded spectrum and classification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Disappearing Ink Trick
activities_projects.objective: Use neutralization and indicators to create 'magic' ink
steps: Mix a few drops of phenolphthalein with the base to turn it pink
Spray the pink liquid onto a white cloth
Observe as the pink color fades as it reacts with CO2 in the air (becoming neutral/acidic)
Explain the chemical transition
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the effect of atmospheric CO2 on basic solutions
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific explanation of the 'disappearing' process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Titration: Finding the Neutral Point
activities_projects.objective: Determine the exact amount of base needed to neutralize an acid
steps: Place 10 drops of vinegar in a cup with turmeric
Add baking soda solution drop by drop
Count exactly how many drops are needed to change the color permanently
Repeat three times to find the average
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Introduces the concept of quantitative chemical reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision in drop counting and data recording (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Soil pH Testing
activities_projects.objective: Analyze soil quality for gardening purposes
steps: Mix soil with distilled water and stir well
Filter the mixture to get a clear liquid
Test the liquid with red and blue litmus
Determine if the soil is acidic, basic, or neutral
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies chemical testing to agriculture and environment
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct analysis of soil samples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Corrosive Nature Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Observe the effect of acids on different materials
steps: Place each material in a separate container of vinegar
Leave overnight and observe changes (bubbles, dissolving, or no reaction)
Tabulate which materials react with acids
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand the chemical reactivity and corrosive property of acids
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed observation report on material degradation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Conductivity of Electrolytes
activities_projects.objective: Test if acidic and basic solutions conduct electricity
materials: Salt water, Lemon juice, Sugar water
steps: Create a simple circuit with an open gap
Dip the wire ends into lemon juice and observe the LED
Compare the brightness with sugar water (non-electrolyte)
Explain why acids/bases conduct electricity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates chemical properties to electrical conductivity (ions)
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful circuit completion and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Effect of Temperature on Neutralization
activities_projects.objective: Investigate if heat speeds up a neutralization reaction
steps: Add a fixed amount of baking soda to cold vinegar and time the fizzing
Repeat with warm vinegar
Analyze which reaction was faster and why
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explores the relationship between temperature and reaction rate
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate timing and logical conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Acidic vs. Basic Fruits Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Compare the acidity of various citrus and non-citrus fruits
steps: Extract juice/pulp from three different fruits
Test each with blue litmus paper
Rank the fruits from most acidic to least acidic based on the speed/intensity of color change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates organic acid concentrations in food
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical ranking based on experimental evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Making 'Milk of Magnesia' at Home
activities_projects.objective: Create a mild base through a simple reaction
steps: Burn magnesium to get Magnesium Oxide (white ash)
Dissolve ash in water to form Magnesium Hydroxide
Test the solution with an indicator to prove it is basic
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the formation of bases from metallic oxides
activities_projects.evaluation: Safe execution and correct identification of the product (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Neutralization Logic Puzzle
activities_projects.objective: Solve word problems based on concentration and volume
steps: Prepare a scenario where a lake is too acidic for fish
Calculate how many 'units' of lime (base) are needed if 1 unit treats 100 liters
Discuss the environmental impact of over-neutralization
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies mathematical logic to chemical problems
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculations and environmental reasoning (10 marks)
